mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon. This reflects a healthy economic environment where consumer spending fuels corporate profitability.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns show no significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate below a certain threshold signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and strong consumer spending, boosting equity valuations and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE falling below 5.0% is consistently followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 40% of occurrences over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is also low, it signals an accommodative monetary environment. This reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, leading to positive equity returns over a 20-day horizon as market participants price in future growth.
regime_hint: low inflation, low interest rate environment
failure_condition: If periods where CPIAUCSL is below 2.0% and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0% do not consistently result in positive S&P 500 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth, a sudden deceleration indicates potential supply chain issues or weakening demand for goods, leading to short-term negative market sentiment and S&P 500 declines over a 5-day horizon as investors react quickly to changing conditions.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 1-month growth rate declines by more than 0.5 percentage points
failure_condition: If a significant deceleration in INDPRO growth during an expansionary regime is followed by positive S&P 500 5-day returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which directly supports consumer spending and corporate profits. This fundamental strength leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a longer 60-day horizon as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns of the S&P 500 show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in housing starts (HOUST) above a certain level signals robust economic activity and consumer confidence, particularly in the housing sector. This translates into broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 performance over a 20-day horizon as related industries benefit.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 1500 thousand units (3-month average)
failure_condition: If HOUST exceeding 1500 thousand units is not followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in at least 60% of observed instances over a 7-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is low and GDP growth (GDPC1) is accelerating, it indicates a supportive monetary policy combined with strengthening economic fundamentals. This combination is highly bullish for equities, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If periods of FEDFUNDS below 1.0% and GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth accelerating do not consistently lead to positive S&P 500 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a significant decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals worsening consumer sentiment and spending. This leads to immediate negative market reactions and S&P 500 declines over a 5-day horizon as investors anticipate lower corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 1-month growth rate declines by more than 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: If a sharp decline in RSAFS during a contractionary regime is followed by positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 30% of cases over a 5-year period.
